title: https://github.com/ShunsukeHayashi/agent-skill-bus FUGUE/Kernelに必要か、批判的吟味せよ
summary_text: **Findings (independent critique)** - **High:** `provider-state.json` as a mutable snapshot is a race hotspot if multiple bridge executions overlap. Require single-writer semantics plus atomic write (`tmp -> rename`), and treat `provider-diffs.jsonl` as append-only truth. - **High:** Fallback metadata is often under-specified. Minimum required per run: `requested_provider`, `actual_provider`, `failed_over_from`, `fallback_reason` (exact error), `attempt`, `timeout_ms`, `timed_out`, `failure_class`, `retryable`. - **High:** Add correlation keys from day 1: `schema_version`, `run_id`, `session_id`, `turn_id`, `lane_family`, `phase`, `role`, `provider`, `model`, `started_at`, `finished_at`, `duration_ms`, `ok`. - **Medium:** Do not store full raw `stdout/stderr` in ledgers by default; that creates privacy and size regressions. Keep bounded summaries + path to full artifact. - **Medium:** Diff generation must be deterministic (`no change => no new diff entry`) to avoid alert spam and false drift. **Regression risks** - Duplicate writes across `active-model-lanes.jsonl` and new `lane-runs.jsonl` without a clear ownership contract. - Back-compat breaks in existing tests/parsers if field names are introduced ad hoc. - Policy drift if fallback is logged but quorum invalidity (`Codex + only one non-Codex`) is not explicitly flagged. **Minimum tests to block regressions** - Concurrent bridge runs writing snapshot/diff files. - Fallback path capture with exact error string preserved. - No-op run produces zero diff entries. - Corrupt/missing state files recover safely. - Quorum-policy flagging test for invalid single-non-Codex adjudication.
